description: PyJWT is a Python library which allows you to encode and decode JSON Web Tokens (JWT). JWT is an open, industry-standard (RFC 7519) for representing claims securely between two parties.

documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `cd toolboxes/pyjwt && python3 pyjwt.py --target $KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST`. Further invocations from upstream: `cd toolboxes/pyjwt && python3 pyjwt.py --help`; `cd toolboxes/pyjwt && python3 pyjwt.py --help`. JSON Web Token encoder/decoder.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the web category. Fits when the engagement needs: JSON Web Token encoder/decoder.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: JSON Web Token encoder/decoder.

name: pyjwt

trust.notes: Operator-curated Python library. The pip name is resolved from PyPI; the import name is verified against the registry.
